SEA-eco
  • API Reference
  • View page source

API Reference

sea_eco lives inside the pySEA namespace. The modules below are the primary entry points for the data structures, processing mixins, and public APIs.

Convenience imports for the SEA-eco package.

Core numpy-based SEA-eco data structures and serialization helpers.

class pySEA.sea_eco.architecture.base_structure_numpy.Dimension(dimension: Dict | Metadata | None = None, name: str = 'Unnamed Dimension', space: Literal['position', 'scattering', 'temporal', 'spectral'] | None = None, scale: float | int | Iterable[float | int] | None = None, offset: float | int | Iterable[float | int] | None = None, size: int | Iterable[int] | None = None, units: str | Iterable[str] = '', values: ndarray[tuple[Any, ...], dtype[Any]] | Iterable[Any] = None)[source]

Bases: SEASerializable

Calibrated axis descriptor for SEA signals.

Tracks axis name, units, scale, offset, optional explicit coordinate values, and semantic role (navigation vs signal; position/scattering/temporal/ spectral). Dimensions are serializable to SEA HDF5.

get_calibrated_value(index, direction='both')[source]

Convert index/indices to calibrated coordinate(s).

get_extent()[source]

Return plotting extents for the axis.

to_hdf5_group(parent_group, force_datasets=None, name=None)[source]

Serialize the dimension into an HDF5 group.

find_nearest_index(value: float, direction: Literal['boht', 'above', 'below'] = 'both', warn_bounds=True) → int[source]

Find the index of the nearest calibrated value.

Parameters:

  • value (float) – Calibrated value to find the nearest index to.

  • direction (Literal['both','above','below']) – Direction to resolve ties. ‘both’ returns nearest, ‘above’ returns next higher index, ‘below’ returns next lower index. Default is ‘both’.

  • warn_bounds (bool, optional) – Warn if the nearest index is at the bounds of the axis, by default True.

Returns:

The nearest index.

Return type:

int

get_calibrated_value(indices: int | Iterable[int]) → float[source]

Get calibrated value at a specific index.

Parameters:

indices (int | Iterable[int]) – Indices in axis array.

Returns:

Value at the specified index.

Return type:

float

Raises:

IndexError – Out of range index.

get_extent() → List[float] | List[Tuple[float]][source]

Get the extent of the dimension for plotting.

Returns:

Extent as [min, max].

Return type:

List[float]

get_tree_html(recursive_level: int = 0, exclude_keys: List[str] = [], exclude_hidden: bool = True, exclude_properties: bool = False, promote_itterable_keys: List[str] = []) → str[source]
property name
property ndim
to_dict(hidden: bool = False, properties: bool = True, exclude_keys: List[str] = [], deep: bool = True)[source]

Recursively convert Dimension object to a dictionary.

Parameters:

  • hidden (bool, optional) – Include hidden attributes (starting with ‘_’), by default False.

  • properties (bool, optional) – Include properties, by default True.

  • exclude_keys (List[str], optional) – Keys to exclude from the dictionary, by default [].

  • deep (bool, optional) – Recursively convert nested SEASerializable objects, by default True.

Returns:

Dictionary representation of the Dimension object.

Return type:

dict

to_hdf5_group(parent_group: File | Group, force_datasets: List = ['_values'], name: str | None = None) → None[source]

Save object to SEA formated HDF5 group.

to_sea(file_path: str, force_datasets: List = ['_values']) → None[source]

Save dimension to HDF5.

property values
class pySEA.sea_eco.architecture.base_structure_numpy.Dimensions(dimensions: Iterable[Dict | Dimension] = [], nav_dimensions: List[int] = [], sig_dimensions: List[int] = [])[source]

Bases: SEASerializable

Ordered collection of calibrated Dimension objects.

Maintains navigation/signal role bookkeeping, provides name/index mapping, and supplies convenience properties for spectral/temporal/position/ scattering axes.

get_dims_as_int(dims)[source]

Normalize dimension references (names/indices) to integer indices.

get_extents(kind='Default')[source]

Return extents suitable for plotting.

add_dimension(dimension, position=None)[source]

Insert a new dimension and update bookkeeping.

remove_dimension(key)[source]

Remove a dimension by name or index.

add_dimension(dimension: Dict | Dimension) → None[source]

Add an dimension to the Dimensions object.

Parameters:

dimension (Dict | Dimension) – Dictionry with dimension calibrations or Dimension class to add.

Raises:

TypeError – If the provided dimension is not a dictionary or Dimension object.

get_dims_as_int(dims: str | int | Iterable[str | int] | None) → List | int[source]

Convert named or integer indices to integers.

Parameters:

dims (str | int | Iterable[str | int] | None) – The index to convert.

Returns:

List of integer indicies

Return type:

List | int

Raises:

  • IndexError – _description_

  • IndexError – _description_

get_extents(kind: Literal['Axes', 'Image'] = 'Axes') → List[float, List[float], List[Tuple[float]]][source]

Get the extents of all dimensions for plotting.

Returns:

List of extents as [min, max] or list of (min, max) tuples for multi-D dimensions.

Return type:

List[float, Tuple[float]]

get_index_from_name(name: str) → int[source]
get_names() → List[str][source]
get_tree_html(recursive_level: int = 0, exclude_keys: List[str] = [], exclude_hidden: bool = True, exclude_properties: bool = False, promote_itterable_keys: List[str] = []) → str[source]
property ndim: int

Get the total number of dimensions across all dimensions.

property position_dimensions: List
remove_dimension(dimension: int | str)[source]
property scattering_dimensions: List
property spectral_dimension: int
property temporal_dimension: int
to_dict(hidden: bool = False, properties: bool = True, exclude_keys: List[str] = [], deep: bool = True)[source]

Recursively convert Dimension object to a dictionary.

Parameters:

  • hidden (bool, optional) – Include hidden attributes (starting with ‘_’), by default False.

  • properties (bool, optional) – Include properties, by default True.

  • exclude_keys (List[str], optional) – Keys to exclude from the dictionary, by default [].

  • deep (bool, optional) – Recursively convert nested SEASerializable objects, by default True.

Returns:

Dictionary representation of the Dimension object.

Return type:

dict

to_hdf5_group(parent_group: File | Group, force_datasets: List = [], name: str | None = None) → None[source]

Save object to SEA formated HDF5 group.

to_sea(file_path: str, force_datasets: List = []) → None[source]

Save dimension to HDF5.

class pySEA.sea_eco.architecture.base_structure_numpy.Metadata(meta: dict | None = None)[source]

Bases: SEASerializable

Hierarchical metadata container used across SEA objects.

update_from_dict(dictionary)[source]

Recursively populate nested metadata entries from dictionaries.

merge(other, kind='skip', warn_duplicate=False, inform_new=False)[source]

Combine two metadata trees with configurable overwrite/append behavior.

to_hdf5_group(parent_group, force_datasets=None, name='Metadata')[source]

Persist metadata into an HDF5 group.

merge(other: Metadata, kind: Literal['overwrite', 'append', 'skip'] = 'skip', warn_duplicate: bool = False, inform_new=False) → None[source]

Merge another Metadata object into this one.

Parameters:

  • other (Metadata) – The other Metadata object to merge.

  • kind (bool, optional) – If True, existing keys will be overwritten. Default is False.

  • warn (bool, optional) – If True, warn when duplicates arise. Default is False.

to_hdf5_group(parent_group, force_datasets=[], name='Metadata')[source]

Save object to SEA formated HDF5 group.

update_from_dict(dictionary: Dict[str, Dict | List | Any | None]) → None[source]

Recursively update Metadata object from a dictionary.

Parameters:

dictionary (dict) – The key will define the Node name. If the value is an empty dictionary the value will be asigned as None. If the value is a dictionary then the value will be another Metadata object. Otherwise the value will be asigned directly.

class pySEA.sea_eco.architecture.base_structure_numpy.SEAFile(metadata: Dict | Metadata | None = None, simulations: SignalCollection | Sequence[Signal | SignalSet] | None = None, experiments: SignalCollection | Sequence[Signal | SignalSet] | None = None, analysis: SignalCollection | Sequence[Signal | SignalSet] | None = None, name: str | None = None, uuid: str | None = None)[source]

Bases: SEASerializable

Top-level SEA container that mirrors the contents of a .sea file.

Bundles simulations, experiments, and analysis collections alongside global metadata; provides helpers to merge detector metadata into signals.

add_simulation(dataset, merge_metadata=False, meta_kwargs=None)[source]

Add a dataset to the Simulations collection.

add_experiment(dataset, merge_metadata=False, meta_kwargs=None)[source]

Add a dataset to the Experiments collection.

add_analysis(dataset, merge_metadata=False, meta_kwargs=None)[source]

Add a dataset to the Analysis collection.

add(obj, location=None, merge_metadata=False, meta_kwargs=None)[source]

Generic adder for signals, sets, collections, or metadata.

remove_simulation(key), remove_experiment(key), remove_analysis(key)[source]

Remove datasets by name or index from the respective collections.

deepcopy()[source]

Deep copy the SEAFile with a fresh UUID.

get_tree_html(...)[source]

Render an HTML tree view promoting SEA collections.

add(obj: SEASerializable, location: Literal['Simulations', 'Experiments', 'Analysis', 'metadata'] | None = None, merge_metadata: bool = False, meta_kwargs: dict[str, Any] | None = None) → None[source]

Generic adder that dispatches to a SEA collection or metadata.

Parameters:

  • obj (SEASerializable) – Object to add (Signal, SignalSet, SignalCollection, or Metadata).

  • location ({'Simulations', 'Experiments', 'Analysis', 'metadata'} | None, optional) – Target destination. If None, destination is inferred when possible; otherwise required.

  • merge_metadata (bool, optional) – Whether to merge dataset metadata into SEAFile metadata (for datasets). Defaults to False.

  • meta_kwargs (dict[str, Any] | None, optional) – Arguments forwarded to Metadata.merge(). Defaults to a safe append behaviour when None.

Raises:

  • TypeError – If the object type is unsupported or metadata destination is inconsistent.

  • ValueError – If the destination cannot be inferred.

Return type:

None

add_analysis(dataset: Signal | SignalSet, merge_metadata: bool = False, meta_kwargs: dict[str, Any] | None = None) → None[source]

Add a dataset to the Analysis collection.

Parameters:

  • dataset (Signal | SignalSet) – Dataset to add.

  • merge_metadata (bool, optional) – Whether to merge dataset metadata into SEAFile metadata. Defaults to False.

  • meta_kwargs (dict[str, Any] | None, optional) – Arguments forwarded to Metadata.merge(). Defaults to a safe append behaviour when None.

Return type:

None

add_experiment(dataset: Signal | SignalSet, merge_metadata: bool = False, meta_kwargs: dict[str, Any] | None = None) → None[source]

Add a dataset to the Experiments collection.

Parameters:

  • dataset (Signal | SignalSet) – Dataset to add.

  • merge_metadata (bool, optional) – Whether to merge dataset metadata into SEAFile metadata. Defaults to False.

  • meta_kwargs (dict[str, Any] | None, optional) – Arguments forwarded to Metadata.merge(). Defaults to a safe append behaviour when None.

Return type:

None

add_simulation(dataset: Signal | SignalSet, merge_metadata: bool = False, meta_kwargs: dict[str, Any] | None = None) → None[source]

Add a dataset to the Simulations collection.

Parameters:

  • dataset (Signal | SignalSet) – Dataset to add.

  • merge_metadata (bool, optional) – Whether to merge dataset metadata into SEAFile metadata. Defaults to False.

  • meta_kwargs (dict[str, Any] | None, optional) – Arguments forwarded to Metadata.merge(). Defaults to a safe append behaviour when None.

Return type:

None

deepcopy()[source]

Create a deep copy of the SEAFile with a fresh UUID.

Returns:

A deep-copied SEAFile instance.

Return type:

SEAFile

get_tree_html(recursive_level: int = 0, exclude_keys: List[str] = [], exclude_hidden: bool = True, exclude_properties: bool = False, promote_itterable_keys: List[str] = []) → str[source]

HTML tree representation that promotes SEA collections.

Parameters:

  • recursive_level (int, optional) – Depth to expand. 0 expands root only. Defaults to 0.

  • exclude_keys (list of str, optional) – Attributes to omit. Defaults to [].

  • exclude_hidden (bool, optional) – Whether to skip hidden attributes. Defaults to True.

  • exclude_properties (bool, optional) – Whether to skip properties. Defaults to False.

  • promote_itterable_keys (list of str, optional) – Iterable attribute names to render as tree branches. Defaults to []; SEA collection names are always added.

Returns:

HTML string representation of the tree.

Return type:

str

classmethod load(file_path: str) → SEAFile[source]

Create a SEAFile from a SEA-formatted file.

Parameters:

file_path (str) – Path to a .sea file.

Returns:

Loaded SEAFile instance.

Return type:

SEAFile

remove(obj: SEASerializable | str | int, location: Literal['Simulations', 'Experiments', 'Analysis', 'metadata'] | None = None) → SEASerializable | None[source]

Remove an object (or first matching name/index) from the SEA file.

Parameters:

  • obj (SEASerializable | str | int) – Dataset instance, name, index, or Metadata.

  • location ({'Simulations', 'Experiments', 'Analysis', 'metadata'} | None, optional) – Target scope. When None, searches collections first then metadata.

Returns:

The removed object.

Return type:

SEASerializable | None

Raises:

ValueError – If the object cannot be found or removal is invalid for the target.

Returns:

The removed object.

Return type:

SEASerializable | None

remove_analysis(key: int | str) → Signal | SignalSet[source]

Remove and return a dataset from Analysis by index or name.

Parameters:

key (int | str) – Dataset index or name.

Returns:

The removed dataset.

Return type:

Signal | SignalSet

remove_experiment(key: int | str) → Signal | SignalSet[source]

Remove and return a dataset from Experiments by index or name.

Parameters:

key (int | str) – Dataset index or name.

Returns:

The removed dataset.

Return type:

Signal | SignalSet

remove_simulation(key: int | str) → Signal | SignalSet[source]

Remove and return a dataset from Simulations by index or name.

Parameters:

key (int | str) – Dataset index or name.

Returns:

The removed dataset.

Return type:

Signal | SignalSet

class pySEA.sea_eco.architecture.base_structure_numpy.SEASerializable[source]

Bases: ABC

Base class for objects that can be serialized to/from HDF5.

Provides shared helpers for converting objects to dictionaries, writing/reading SEA-formatted HDF5 files, and rendering tree views for inspection.

to_dict(hidden=True, properties=False, exclude_keys=None, deep=True)[source]

Recursively convert the object to a nested dictionary.

to_hdf5_group(parent_group, force_datasets=None, name=None, exclude_keys=None)[source]

Persist the object into an HDF5 group with SEA metadata.

to_sea(file_path, force_datasets=None)[source]

Serialize into a standalone *.sea file.

from_sea(file_path)[source]

Populate the instance from a *.sea file.

from_hdf5_group(group)[source]

Populate the instance from an existing HDF5 group.

get_tree_html(...)[source]

Render an HTML representation of the object tree.

deepcopy()[source]

Create a deep copy of the object.

ensure_uuid() → UUID[source]

Ensure self exposes a UUID attribute and return it as uuid.UUID.

find_attribute(attr_name: str, return_first: bool = False, include_hidden: bool = True) → Dict[str, Any][source]

Recursively search self for attributes named attr_name.

Returns:

Mapping of dotted/bracketed paths to the matching attribute values. If return_first is True, traversal stops after the first match (depth-first order). If include_hidden is False, attributes/keys starting with _ are skipped.

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

from_hdf5_group(group: Group)[source]

Populate the current object from an HDF5 group.

from_sea(file_path: str)[source]

Load object data from an HDF5 file.

get_tree_html(recursive_level: int = 0, exclude_keys: List[str] = [], exclude_hidden: bool = True, exclude_properties: bool = False, promote_itterable_keys: List[str] = []) → str[source]
get_tree_str(pad: str = '', recursive_level=None)[source]

Print class as a tree.

Parameters:

  • pad (str, optional) – What to add before each entry, by default ‘’

  • recursive_level (int, optional) – What depth to stop recursing. Not implemented, by default 0

Returns:

_description_

Return type:

_type_

get_uuid_recursive() → UUID | None[source]

Return a UUID extracted recursively from self if available.

save(file_path: str) → None[source]
show_tree(show: Literal['html', 'str'] | None = 'html', recursive_level=0)[source]
to_dict(hidden: bool = True, properties: bool = False, exclude_keys: List[str] = [], deep: bool = True)[source]

Recursively convert Dimension object to a dictionary.

Parameters:

  • hidden (bool, optional) – Include hidden attributes (starting with ‘_’), by default Truee.

  • properties (bool, optional) – Include properties, by default False.

  • exclude_keys (List[str], optional) – Keys to exclude from the dictionary, by default [].

  • deep (bool, optional) – Recursively convert nested SEASerializable objects, by default True.

Returns:

Dictionary representation of the Dimension object.

Return type:

dict

to_hdf5_group(parent_group: File | Group, force_datasets: List = [], name: str | None = None, exclude_keys: List[str] = []) → None[source]

Save object to SEA formated HDF5 group.

to_sea(file_path: str, force_datasets: List = []) → None[source]

Save object to SEA formated HDF5.

class pySEA.sea_eco.architecture.base_structure_numpy.Signal(data: ndarray[tuple[Any, ...], dtype[_ScalarT]] | None = None, name: str = 'Signal', uuid: str = 'f34ccefd-114c-4feb-aaa6-990097f94388', dimensions: Dimensions | List | None = None, signal_type: Literal['2D-EELS', '1D-EELS', 'Diffraction', 'Image'] | None = None, dimensions_domain: Literal['local', 'global'] = 'local', original_metadata: Metadata | None = None, is_lazy: bool = False, metadata: Dict | Metadata | None = None)[source]

Bases: SEASerializable, SignalProcessingMixin

N-dimensional signal with calibrated dimensions and metadata.

Wraps ndarray data with Dimensions, Metadata, and plotting/processing helpers while preserving provenance (UUIDs) and SEA serialization support.

deepcopy_with_new_data(data)[source]

Clone the signal, replacing the underlying data.

deepcopy_with_reduced_data_dim(data, keep_dim)[source]

Clone the signal while adjusting dimensions after reduction.

show(...), image(...)[source]

Plot or export arrays with calibrated axes.

to_sea(file_path, force_datasets=None)[source]

Serialize to SEA HDF5.

bind_dimensions(dims: Dimensions) → None[source]

Bind the signal to shared dimensions.

Parameters:

dims (Dimensions) – Shared dimensions object to reference live.

Notes

Binding sets dimensions_domain to 'global' so future mutations to the shared Dimensions are reflected in this signal.

deepcopy()[source]

Copy the signal

deepcopy_with_new_data(data: ndarray)[source]

Copy the signal with new data.

deepcopy_with_reduced_data_dim(data: ndarray, keep_dim: Tuple[int | str]) → Signal[source]

Copy the signal with new data of reduced dimensionality.

detach_dimensions() → None[source]

Detach from shared dimensions by snapshotting the current layout.

Notes

The current resolved dimensions are deep-copied into the local store and dimensions_domain is set to 'local'.

property dimensions

Return the Dimensions currently applied to the Signal.

Returns:

Shared dimensions when the signal is in 'global' mode and bound to a shared object; otherwise the local dimensions. If no shared dimensions are bound while in global mode, the local dimensions are returned as a fallback.

Return type:

Dimensions | None

fold_axes()[source]

Restore data shape from a state produced by unfold_axes.

generate_child_signal(*args, **kwargs)[source]
get_tree_html(recursive_level: int = 0, exclude_keys: List[str] = [], exclude_hidden: bool = True, exclude_properties: bool = False, promote_itterable_keys: List[str] = []) → str[source]
image(dims: None | ~typing.Literal['sig', 'nav'] | ~collections.abc.Iterable[int, str] = None, fnc: ~typing.Callable | None = <function sum>, filename: str | None = None) → None[source]
infer_plot_dims(dims, fnc)[source]

Infer and validate plot dimensions from the given input.

This method determines which dimensions of the data should be plotted based on the input specification. It handles cases where dimensions are not explicitly provided by inferring them from the data structure’s signal or navigation dimensions.

Parameters:

  • dims ({None, 'sig', 'nav', int, tuple of int}, optional) – Specification of dimensions to plot: - None: Automatically infer dimensions from data structure - ‘sig’: Use signal dimensions if <= 2D - ‘nav’: Use navigation dimensions if <= 2D - int: Single dimension index - tuple of int: Explicit dimension indices Default is None.

  • fnc (callable) – Function to apply for reducing remaining dimensions. Typically used for averaging or slicing over non-plotted dimensions.

Returns:

  • data (BaseStructureNumpy) – The data object, possibly reduced by fnc if dimensions remain after selecting plot dimensions.

  • dims (tuple of int) – The validated dimension indices to be plotted, converted to integer indices relative to the data structure.

Raises:

  • ValueError – If the inferred or specified dimensions exceed 2D, which is not yet implemented for plotting.

  • ValueError – If dims cannot be inferred because total, signal, and navigation dimensions are all larger than 2D.

Notes

The method automatically reduces the dataset along non-plotted dimensions using the provided function if remaining dimensions exist.

property original_metadata: Metadata
show(ax: ~matplotlib.axes._axes.Axes | None = None, dims: None | ~typing.Literal['sig', 'nav'] | ~collections.abc.Iterable[int, str] = None, fnc: ~typing.Callable | None = <function sum>, filename: str | None = None, **kwargs) → PlotImage | None[source]

A shortcut plotting function that automatically handles things like dimensionality and matplotlib kwargs.

Parameters:

  • ax (mplAxes | None, optional) – Axis to plot to. If None then an axis is both created and returned, by default None

  • dims (None | Literal['sig','nav'] | Iterable[int, str], optional) – Specification of dimensions to plot - None: Automatically infer dimensions from data structure - ‘sig’: Use signal dimensions if <= 2D - ‘nav’: Use navigation dimensions if <= 2D - int: Single dimension index - tuple of int: Explicit dimension indices Default is None.

  • fnc (Callable | None, optional) – Function used to collapse the not dims dimensions, by default np.sum

  • filename (str | None, optional) – File to save the plot to, by default None

  • **kwarg – kwargs supplied to imshow or plot.

Returns:

plot – Generated object that was plotted in the axis.

Return type:

PlotImage | None

Raises:

NotImplementedError – Plotting is implemented for multivariate dimensions but not yet for multivariate signals.

to_dict(hidden: bool = False, properties: bool = True, exclude_keys: List[str] = ['data'], deep: bool = True)[source]

Recursively convert Dimension object to a dictionary.

Parameters:

  • hidden (bool, optional) – Include hidden attributes (starting with ‘_’), by default False.

  • properties (bool, optional) – Include properties, by default True.

  • exclude_keys (List[str], optional) – Keys to exclude from the dictionary, by default [].

  • deep (bool, optional) – Recursively convert nested SEASerializable objects, by default True.

Returns:

Dictionary representation of the Dimension object.

Return type:

dict

to_hdf5_group(parent_group: File | Group, force_datasets: List = ['data'], name: str | None = None) → None[source]

Save the signal to an HDF5 group.

Parameters:

  • parent_group (File or Group) – HDF5 file or group to write into.

  • force_datasets (list, optional) – Attributes to coerce into datasets, by default ['data'].

  • name (str or None, optional) – Group name override, by default None.

Notes

When the signal is using shared dimensions (dimensions_domain == 'global'), the dimensions are deep-copied into the local store for serialization so the exported signal remains self-contained. The runtime-only shared binding (_dimensions_shared) is excluded from serialization.

to_sea(file_path: str, force_datasets: List = ['data']) → None[source]

Save dimension to HDF5.

unfold_axes(keep_axes: Sequence[int | str] = []) → dict[source]

Flatten all axes except those listed in keep_axes. Example: shape (l,m,n,o), keep_axes=(1,2) -> (l*o, m, n); keep_axes=(2,) -> (l*m*o, n). Returns a state dict for fold_axes.

class pySEA.sea_eco.architecture.base_structure_numpy.SignalCollection(datasets: Sequence[Signal | SignalSet] | None = None, metadata: Dict | Metadata | None = None, name: str | None = None, uuid: str | None = None)[source]

Bases: SEASerializable

Collection of Signals and/or SignalSets with shared metadata.

add_dataset(dataset, merge_metadata=False, merge_dimensions=False, meta_kwargs=None)[source]

Append a dataset, optionally merging metadata/dimensions.

remove_dataset(key)[source]

Remove a dataset by index or name.

to_sea(file_path)

Persist the collection to a SEA file.

load(file_path)[source]

Classmethod to build a collection from disk.

add_dataset(dataset: Signal | SignalSet, merge_metadata: bool = False, name: str | None = None, meta_kwargs: dict[str, Any] | None = None) → None[source]

Add a dataset to the collection.

Parameters:

  • dataset (Signal | SignalSet) – Dataset to add.

  • merge_metadata (bool, optional) – Merge dataset metadata into the collection metadata, by default False.

  • meta_kwargs (dict[str, Any] | None, optional) – Keyword arguments forwarded to Metadata.merge, by default dict(kind='append', warn_duplicate=False, inform_new=False).

allowed_types: tuple[type, ...] = (<class 'pySEA.sea_eco.architecture.base_structure_numpy.Signal'>, <class 'pySEA.sea_eco.architecture.base_structure_numpy.SignalSet'>)
deepcopy()[source]

Deep copy the collection and refresh its UUID.

Returns:

Deep-copied collection.

Return type:

SignalCollection

get_dataset_names() → List[str][source]

Return dataset names.

Returns:

Names of contained datasets.

Return type:

list[str]

get_index_from_name(name: str) → int[source]

Resolve a dataset name to its index.

Parameters:

name (str) – Dataset name.

Returns:

Index of the dataset.

Return type:

int

Raises:

KeyError – If the name is not found.

get_tree_html(recursive_level: int = 0, exclude_keys: List[str] = [], exclude_hidden: bool = True, exclude_properties: bool = False, promote_itterable_keys: List[str] = []) → str[source]

HTML tree representation that promotes datasets.

Parameters:

  • recursive_level (int, optional) – Depth to expand, by default 0.

  • exclude_keys (list[str], optional) – Attributes to omit, by default [].

  • exclude_hidden (bool, optional) – Exclude hidden attributes, by default True.

  • exclude_properties (bool, optional) – Exclude properties, by default False.

  • promote_itterable_keys (list[str], optional) – Iterable attributes to promote, by default [].

Returns:

HTML representation.

Return type:

str

classmethod load(file_path: str) → SignalCollection[source]

Create a SignalCollection from a SEA file.

Parameters:

file_path (str) – Path to the SEA file.

Returns:

Loaded collection.

Return type:

SignalCollection

remove_dataset(key: int | str) → Signal | SignalSet[source]

Remove and return a dataset by index or name.

Parameters:

key (int | str) – Dataset index or name.

Returns:

Removed dataset.

Return type:

Signal | SignalSet

class pySEA.sea_eco.architecture.base_structure_numpy.SignalSet(signals: Sequence[Signal] | None = None, main_signal: int | None = None, name: str | None = None, metadata: Dict | Metadata | None = None, dimensions: Dimensions | None = None, uuid: str | None = None, merge_metadata: bool = False)[source]

Bases: SignalCollection

Specialized collection that stores Signals with shared Dimensions.

add_dataset(dataset, merge_metadata=False, meta_kwargs=None)[source]

Append a signal while binding shared dimensions and optionally merging metadata.

decompose_metadata(standalone=True)[source]

Split common vs detector-specific metadata and optionally attach per-signal copies.

from_acquisition(signals, name=None, metadata=None, dimensions=None, enforce_aquisition_uuid=False, merge_metadata=True)[source]

Classmethod factory for acquisition-style sets with optional UUID enforcement.

add_dataset(dataset: Signal, merge_metadata: bool = False, meta_kwargs: dict[str, Any] | None = None) → None[source]

Add a Signal to the set, binding shared dimensions.

Parameters:

  • dataset (Signal) – Signal to add.

  • merge_metadata (bool, optional) – Merge signal metadata into set metadata, by default False.

  • meta_kwargs (dict[str, Any] | None, optional) – Keyword arguments forwarded to Metadata.merge.

allowed_types: tuple[type, ...] = (<class 'pySEA.sea_eco.architecture.base_structure_numpy.Signal'>,)
decompose_metadata(standalone: bool = True) → tuple[Metadata | None, dict[str, Metadata]][source]

Decompose set metadata into common and detector-specific parts.

Parameters:

standalone (bool, optional) – When True, assign each signal a standalone Metadata object combining common metadata with its detector-specific metadata. When False, signals have their metadata cleared but detector-specific metadata is still returned. Defaults to True.

Returns:

Common metadata and a mapping of detector name to Metadata.

Return type:

tuple[Metadata | None, dict[str, Metadata]]

classmethod from_acquisition(signals: Sequence[Signal] | None = None, name: str | None = None, metadata: Dict | Metadata | None = None, dimensions: Dimensions | None = None, enforce_aquisition_uuid: bool = False, merge_metadata: bool = True) → SignalSet[source]

Factory for acquisition-style SignalSets.

Parameters:

  • signals (Sequence[Signal] | None, optional) – Signals to include.

  • name (str | None, optional) – Optional name.

  • metadata (Metadata | Dict | None, optional) – Set-level metadata.

  • dimensions (Dimensions | None, optional) – Shared dimensions.

  • enforce_aquisition_uuid (bool, optional) – If True, ensure all signals share the same acquisition UUID and use it for the set UUID. Defaults to False.

  • merge_metadata (bool, optional) – Merge signal metadata into the set metadata on add. Defaults to True.

Returns:

Constructed acquisition-style set.

Return type:

SignalSet

pySEA.sea_eco.architecture.base_structure_numpy.ask_to_proceed() → None[source]

Prompt for user confirmation in interactive flows.

The function blocks until a Y/N (or empty for Y) response is entered, printing a short status before returning.

Return type:

None

Notes

Intended for CLI/debug workflows; avoid in library code.

pySEA.sea_eco.architecture.base_structure_numpy.generate_uuid() → str[source]

Create a random UUID string.

Returns:

Newly generated UUID4 identifier.

Return type:

str

pySEA.sea_eco.architecture.base_structure_numpy.get_index_or_none(list_in: List[Any], value: Any) → int | None[source]

Return the index of value in list_in or None if absent.

Parameters:

  • list_in (list) – Container to search.

  • value (Any) – Target value.

Returns:

Index of value or None when not found.

Return type:

int | None

pySEA.sea_eco.architecture.base_structure_numpy.get_property_dict(obj: object) → dict[source]

Return the property attributes defined on a class.

Parameters:

obj (object) – Instance whose class properties should be enumerated.

Returns:

Mapping of property names to their current values.

Return type:

dict

pySEA.sea_eco.architecture.base_structure_numpy.get_tree_html(obj, recursive_level: int = 0, exclude_keys: Sequence[str] | None = None, exclude_hidden: bool = True, exclude_properties: bool = False, promote_itterable_keys: Sequence[str] | None = None) → str[source]

Build an HTML representation of a SEA-eco object’s attribute tree.

Parameters:

  • obj (Any) – Serializable object implementing the SEA interface.

  • recursive_level (int, optional) – Current recursion depth used to control expansion, by default 0.

  • exclude_keys (Sequence[str] | None, optional) – Attribute names to omit from the rendered tree, by default None.

  • exclude_hidden (bool, optional) – Skip attributes starting with _ when True, by default True.

  • exclude_properties (bool, optional) – Skip property attributes when True, by default False.

  • promote_itterable_keys (Sequence[str] | None, optional) – Iterable attributes to expand vertically rather than inline, by default None.

Returns:

HTML unordered list representing the object tree.

Return type:

str

pySEA.sea_eco.architecture.base_structure_numpy.safe_decode(value: Any) → Any[source]

Safely decode bytes/numpy values to Python-native objects.

Parameters:

value (Any) – Raw value (often from HDF5 attrs/datasets) to decode.

Returns:

Decoded Python object; bytes are UTF-8 decoded when possible, numpy types are converted to built-ins.

Return type:

Any

pySEA.sea_eco.architecture.base_structure_numpy.safe_encode(value: Any) → Any[source]

Encode Python values (including numpy types) for HDF5 attributes.

Parameters:

value (Any) – Object to encode for storage.

Returns:

UTF-8 encoded bytes for strings/None or numpy-safe Python types.

Return type:

Any

Signal processing mixins for SEA-eco Signal objects.

class pySEA.sea_eco.mixins.signal_mixins.SignalProcessingMixin[source]

Bases: object

autocorrelate(dims: Sequence[int] | int = -1, normalize: bool = True, kwargs_correlate: dict = {}) → ndarray[tuple[Any, ...], dtype[_ScalarT]][source]

Measure autocorrelation of a signal across specified dimensions.

Parameters:

  • dims (Sequence[int] | int, optional) – Dimension(s) to correlate along, by default -1

  • normalize (bool, optional) – Perform normalized cross-correlation, by default True

  • kwargs_correlate (dict, optional) – Keyword arguments passed to scipy.signal.correlate, by default {}

Returns:

  • lags (list[NDArray]) – Lag arrays for each dimension.

  • corr (NDArray) – Autocorrelation coefficients.

correlate_1D_in_ND(ref_sig: Signal | NDArray, dims: int = -1, normalize: bool = True, kwargs_correlate: dict = {}) → Signal[source]

Perform 1D cross-correlation across a ND signal. If the signal has shifted positive with respect tothe reference then the correlation will return a positive lag.

Parameters:

  • ref_sig (Signal | NDArray) – Refference dataset to correlate against.

  • dims (int, optional) – Dimension to correlate along. By default -1

  • normalize (bool, optional) – Perfrom normalized cross-correlation. By default True.

  • kwargs_correlate (dict, optional) – kwargs for scipy.signal.correlate, by default {}

Returns:

Correlations – Correlation coefficients.

Return type:

Signal

See also

scipy.signal.correlate

get_shifts_autocorrelate(dims: Sequence[int] | int = -1, normalize: bool = True, cumlative: bool = True, kwargs_correlate: dict = {}) → tuple[list[ndarray[tuple[Any, ...], dtype[_ScalarT]]], ndarray[tuple[Any, ...], dtype[_ScalarT]], ndarray[tuple[Any, ...], dtype[_ScalarT]]][source]

Measure autocorrelated shift of a signal across specified dimensions.

Parameters:

  • dims (Sequence[int] | int, optional) – Dimension(s) to correlate along, by default -1

  • normalize (bool, optional) – Perform normalized cross-correlation, by default True

  • cumlative (bool, optional) – Treat the shift as a cumlative shift from the first frame, by default True

  • kwargs_correlate (dict, optional) – Keyword arguments passed to scipy.signal.correlate, by default {}

Returns:

shfit – Autocorrelation shift.

Return type:

NDArray

get_shifts_correlate_1D_in_ND(ref_sig: Signal | NDArray, dims: int = -1, normalize: bool = True, kwargs_correlate: dict | None = None, avg_corr_dims: Sequence[int] | int = [], cumlative: bool = True) → Signal[source]

Perform 1D cross-correlation across a ND signal. If the signal has shifted positive with respect tothe reference then the correlation will return a positive lag.

Parameters:

  • ref_sig (Signal | NDArray) – Refference dataset to correlate against.

  • dims (int, optional) – Dimension to correlate along. By default -1

  • normalize (bool, optional) – Perfrom normalized cross-correlation. By default True.

  • kwargs_correlate (dict, optional) – kwargs for scipy.signal.correlate, by default None

  • cumlative (bool, optional) – Treat the shift as a cumlative shift from the first frame, by default True

Returns:

Correlations – Correlation coefficients.

Return type:

Signal

See also

scipy.signal.correlate

maxND(dims: Sequence | Literal['signal', 'navigation', 'temporal', 'spectral', 'position', 'scattering'] = 'signal', calibrated: bool = True) → ndarray[tuple[Any, ...], dtype[_ScalarT]][source]

Find the maxium value and its index in a set of dimensions.

Parameters:

dims (tuple, optional) – Axes to find the max within, by default (-2,-1)

Returns:

max_values – Maximum values with the shape (*data.shape,len(dims)+1) with non-dims removed. The last dimension coresponds to the dims indicies and the max value.

Return type:

NDArray

shift(shifts: NDArray, shifts_calibrated=True, dims: int | Sequence[int] = -1, interpolate_method: Callable | Literal['linear', 'cubic'] = 'cubic', kwargs_interpolate: dict = {}) → Signal[source]

Shift an array along one or more dimensions using interpolation.

Parameters:

  • shifts (NDArray | Sequence[NDArray | float | int]) – Shift amounts. For multiple dims supply a sequence aligned to dims; each element can be scalar or array broadcastable to the data shape (or broadcastable after expanding singleton dimensions).

  • dims (int | Sequence[int], optional) – Dimension(s) (axis/axes) to shift along, by default -1.

  • interpolate_method (Callable | Literal['linear', 'cubic'], optional) – Interpolation selector. Strings map to standard options; callables are instantiated directly. Defaults to ‘cubic’.

  • kwargs_interpolate (dict | None, optional) – Extra keyword arguments passed to the interpolation routine.

Returns:

Shifted signal.

Return type:

Signal

pySEA.sea_eco.mixins.signal_mixins.get_corr_dimensions(signal: Signal, dims: Sequence[int], lags: NDArray, corr: NDArray) → Dimensions[source]

Update dimension metadata with lag offsets and sizes.

Public signal-processing API for SEA-eco.

Public calculator utilities for SEA-eco.

I/O helpers for SEA-eco signals and metadata.

pySEA.sea_eco.io.collect_swift_file(file_path: str) → tuple[dict[str, Any], ndarray[tuple[Any, ...], dtype[_ScalarT]]][source]

Load Swift-generated data/metadata, handling multiple container types.

pySEA.sea_eco.io.infer_dimensions(dimensions_meta: Metadata | Dict, data_shape: Iterable[int], signal_type: Literal['2D-EELS', '1D-EELS', 'Diffraction', 'Image'] | None = None, small_angle: bool = True) → str | None[source]
pySEA.sea_eco.io.load(file_path: str) → SEASerializable[source]
pySEA.sea_eco.io.load_memmap_from_npz(path: str, name: str) → ndarray[tuple[Any, ...], dtype[_ScalarT]][source]

Load an array inside an npz as a read-only memmap.

Notes

NumPy cannot directly memmap arrays embedded in npz archives; this is a lightweight workaround adapted from https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/5976.

pySEA.sea_eco.io.load_swift_to_sea(file_path: str, signal_type: str = None, lazy: bool = False, print_missed: bool = False, read_aberrations=False, **kwargs) → object[source]
pySEA.sea_eco.io.parse_file_path(file_path: str) → tuple[str, str, str][source]

Split a file path into directory, stem, and extension.

If no extension is present, attempt to infer one matching .n* patterns.

pySEA.sea_eco.io.safe_decode(value: Any) → Any[source]

Safely decode bytes and numpy values to native Python objects.

pySEA.sea_eco.io.swift_to_sea_metadata(swift_metadata: Metadata, signal_type: Literal['2D-EELS', '1D-EELS', 'Diffraction', 'Image'] | None = None, file_path: str = None, print_missed: bool = False, read_aberrations=False) → Metadata[source]

Convert Metadata object constructed from swift metadata to the SEA format of metadata.

Parameters:

  • swift_metadata (Metadata) – Metadata object constructed from swift json metadata that will be converted to the SEA format of metadata.

  • signal_type (Literal['2D-EELS','1D-EELS','Diffraction','Image'] | None, optional) – The type of signal, by default None

  • file_path (str, optional) – File path to the original file, by default None

  • print_missed (bool, optional) – Print if the metadata attribute was not found, by default False

  • read_aberrations (bool, optional) – Read the instrument aberrations, by default False

Returns:

SEA format of metadata

Return type:

Metadata
